phonetic: 藢n蓲nda瑟藞rek蕛蓹n蓹l; 藢n蓲nd蓹藞rek蕛蓹n蓹l
phoneticUs: 藞n蓱藧nd蓹藞rek蕛蓹nl; 藞n蓱藧nda瑟藞rek蕛蓹nl
meaning: n.鏃犳柟鍚戠殑,閫傚悎鍚勬柟鍚戠殑
examples: In existing soundtracks, the system recognizes nondirectional sounds like falling rain and sends them to the new speakers.
A radio beacon which transmits nondirectional signals whereby the pilot can use the ADF to determine his bearing to or from the station.
This is what you call a nondirectional bet.
